summary: Full backend+frontend regression for RRDC GO. Backend: 24/24 pytest tests pass (auth, admin CRUD, pokemon, image upload, spawn config, full spawn+catch flow, bank, analytics, auth guards). Frontend: login, invalid login error, camper redirect to /ar with Camera Needed fallback, /collection page, admin login, all 4 admin tabs (Analytics, Campers, Pokemon 60 slots, Spawns) all functional. Camper creation and spawn config save produce correct toasts and persist.

issues: Login text inputs on dark bg have no visible border; functional but could use subtle outline for accessibility
'Camera Needed / Requested device not found' fallback works as expected in non-camera environments

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: seed_admin compares verify_password(ADMIN_PASSWORD, hash) - good; updates hash when env changes. OK.
server.py allow_credentials=False with allow_origins=* is fine since auth is Bearer-in-header (no cookies).
Brute-force lockout (LOCKOUT_MAX=8, 10min) implemented; /api/auth/login and /api/admin/auth/login both use check_lockout/record_failure/clear_failures. Good.
bcrypt hash_password uses bcrypt.gensalt() -> $2b$ prefix. Verified via pytest round-trip.
maybe_create_spawn correctly lazy-creates spawns per group; respects active_hours and enabled flag; properly expires and resets next_spawn_at.
Input validation: admin PATCH pokemon validates power_level 1-1000; PUT spawn-config validates min<=max and 0<=hours<=24. 409 on duplicate username. Good.
MongoDB _id is excluded in all reads (projection {'_id':0}). No ObjectId leaks observed.
Minor: is_within_active_hours uses .astimezone().hour which reflects server local time - may be confusing when deployed across timezones; consider explicit camp tz.
Minor: /api/admin/users list route excludes password_hash but also excludes 'created_at' in projection {_id:0, password_hash:0}, sort by created_at still works (Mongo uses stored field). OK.
Minor: /api/spawn/catch does not re-check active_hours; if config is disabled mid-spawn, existing spawn can still be caught - acceptable UX.
All interactive UI elements have data-testid (login-*, admin-*, admin-tab-*, user-form-*, spawn-config-save, pokemon-slot-*). Good.
test_credentials: admin/Camp1993 (seeded). Test user red-squirrels/camp123 created and cleaned up. No persistent test data remains.
seed_data_creation: Tests create+delete user 'test-red-squirrels', activate slot 1 as TEST_Legendary for catch flow, then reset to default. Full cleanup performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py is the canonical regression suite. Before running, ensure MongoDB is accessible and no stale 'test-red-squirrels' user exists (the module fixture cleans it). Frontend data-testids to know: login-username-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, admin-username-input, admin-password-input, admin-login-submit, admin-tab-{analytics,users,pokemon,spawns}, user-form-{username,password,group,save}, spawn-config-save, pokemon-slot-*.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All endpoints respond per spec, UI integrates with backend via Bearer tokens in localStorage (rrdc_user_token, rrdc_admin_token). Full catch flow validated end-to-end (POST /admin/pokemon to activate, force group_spawns next_spawn_at into past, GET /spawn/current creates spawn, POST /spawn/catch succeeds, /bank and /catches reflect the catch).
